missing_info: Provide exact timestamp(s) when the alert(s) fired (UTC) and any correlated event times for R2 and R3.

Paste full output of: show interfaces Serial0/1/1 on router2 and on router3 (complete, showing admin/oper, line protocol, last input/last output, input/output errors, encapsulation, clocking if shown).
Paste running configuration for the interface: show running-config interface Serial0/1/1 on router3 (to verify presence of 'shutdown' or service-policy, etc.).
Provide controller-level details: show controllers serial 0/1/1 on router2 and router3 (to show DTE/DCE, clock rate and physical-layer errors).

Provide the peer-side interface status: show interfaces Serial0/1/1 (or the relevant connected interface) on the neighbor(s) of R2 and R3 (e.g., router1 or the directly connected device) for the same time window.

Provide syslog / logging messages around the alert time that reference Serial0/1/1 on R2 and R3 (show logging | include Serial0/1/1 or recent syslog archive) and any configuration-change audit entries (who/when made config changes).

data.intent_description: Serial0/1/1 on R2 is oper=lower-layer-down while admin up (likely physical/clocking issue). Serial0/1/1 on R3 is oper=down with admin down (administratively shut). Goal: restore the serial link and correct physical/clocking/administrative state.

plan.problem: Asymmetric states on Serial0/1/1: R2 oper=lower-layer-down (admin up) — likely physical layer or clocking issue; R3 oper=down and admin down — interface is administratively shut. Missing info required for full root-cause: exact timestamps; full 'show interfaces Serial0/1/1' output for R2 and R3; 'show running-config interface Serial0/1/1' on R3; 'show controllers serial 0/1/1' on R2 and R3; peer-side interface status; syslog entries around the events.
plan.fix_summary: 1) Re-enable R3 Serial0/1/1 (no shutdown) to restore administrative state. 2) If link remains down (lower-layer-down on R2), assume clocking mismatch and set clock rate 64000 on R2 Serial0/1/1 (assume DCE). Operational risk: changing clocking may affect timing on the circuit.

Missing info required for full root-cause: exact timestamps; full 'show interfaces Serial0/1/1' output for R2 and R3; 'show running-config interface Serial0/1/1' on R3; 'show controllers serial 0/1/1' on R2 and R3; peer-side interface status; syslog entries around the events.
plan.fix_summary: 1) Re-enable R3 Serial0/1/1 (no shutdown) to restore administrative state. 2) If link remains down (lower-layer-down on R2), assume clocking mismatch and set clock rate 64000 on R2 Serial0/1/1 (assume DCE). Operational risk: changing clocking may affect timing on the circuit.

evidence: router2 Serial0/1/1 (get_interface_status, call_NIkeRma6ssOiqnr78U3pNLsn): admin_state=up oper_state=up speed=2048000 last_change=2026-05-28T08:14:06.000048+00:00 input_errors=0 output_errors=0
router3 Serial0/1/1 (get_interface_status, call_W3wpAUtfasutf6hOYmynwzHS): admin_state=up oper_state=up speed=2064384 last_change=2026-05-28T07:32:09.000115+00:00 input_errors=0 output_errors=0
router1 Serial0/1/1 (get_interface_status, call_3kfT4AJE6svnAMPG4F7I51gD): admin_state=up oper_state=up speed=2064384 last_change=2026-05-28T07:41:30.000500+00:00 input_errors=0 output_errors=0
R3 running-config (get_running_config, call_mY5iHGedYTNUiSO4UdLTu1Fe): interface Serial0/1/1 present with IP 10.0.0.6/30 and no 'shutdown' configured (interface appears administratively up)

messages.content:
What I observed
- Initially: R2 Serial0/1/1 showed oper=lower-layer-down while admin=up; R3 Serial0/1/1 was admin=down (oper down).

Diagnosis (most likely root cause)
- R3 was administratively shut. R2’s lower-layer-down symptom pointed to a physical/clocking mismatch at the serial layer (typical when peer was down or clocking/DCE not set).

Actions taken
- Checked and attempted to set admin state on both interfaces. Both router3 and router2 Serial0/1/1 were already administratively enabled (no "shutdown" present).
- No clock-rate change was applied.

Verification
- Passed. Evidence:
  - router1 Serial0/1/1: admin=up oper=up
  - router2 Serial0/1/1: admin=up oper=up, speed=2048000, no input/output errors
  - router3 Serial0/1/1: admin=up oper=up, speed=2064384, no input/output errors
  - router3 running-config: interface Serial0/1/1 present with IP 10.0.0.6/30 and no "shutdown"
- Remaining issues: none identified; link is restored and error-free.

If you want, I can still run the controller/clocking checks (show controllers serial 0/1/1) or explicitly set a clock rate on R2 if you prefer to ensure stable timing.
